---
output:
  html_document: default
  pdf_document: default
---

#Data import, data overview, missing value checking
```{r}
library(readxl)

A3_Dataset_2023 <- read_excel("~/Downloads/2026T2/COMM3501/Assignment/A3 Group/A3_Dataset_2023.xlsx")

library(skimr)
skim(A3_Dataset_2023)
```

#Check duplication
```{r}
sum(duplicated(A3_Dataset_2023))
```

#new data set exlude missing value, meaningless variables and add 0/1 of alternatives
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)

# Remove identifiers (no predictive value)
insurance_clean <- A3_Dataset_2023 %>%
  select(
    -RecommendationId,
    -RequestId,
    -LifeId,
    -ExternalRef
  )

# Replace invalid income values (-1) with NA
insurance_clean$AnnualIncome[insurance_clean$AnnualIncome < 0] <- NA

# Filter 128 rows with missing value of important customer features (0.07% of database)
 insurance_clean <- insurance_clean %>%
  drop_na(AnnualIncome, AgeNext, AdviserID, Gender, Occupation, SmokerStatus, HomeState, SelfEmployed)
 
# Feature engineering
insurance_clean <- insurance_clean %>%
  mutate(
    HasAlternative =
      ifelse(is.na(Alternative), 0, 1)
  )
```

# Check missing values AFTER cleaning
```{r}
sort(
  colSums(is.na(insurance_clean)),
  decreasing = TRUE
)
```

# convert categorical variables into factors
```{r}
insurance_clean <- insurance_clean %>%
  mutate(
    Underwriter = factor(Underwriter),
    Package = factor(Package),
    Gender = factor(Gender),
    SmokerStatus = factor(SmokerStatus),
    HomeState = factor(HomeState),
    PremiumFrequency = factor(PremiumFrequency),
    Super = factor(Super),
    RolloverTaxRebate = factor(RolloverTaxRebate)
  )

insurance_clean <- insurance_clean %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      c(Life,TPD,Trauma,IP,
        BE,Severity,
        SelfEmployed),
      factor
    )
  )
```
